pages.raw_text:
The report of the subcommittee of
city fathers appointed to investigate
the Division street subway matter
was handed to the city council at
last nights meeting. This commit
tee and the railroad officials of the
Northern Pacific met on July and
the committee recommended the per-
manent closing of Browne street, re-
grading of Sprague and Division
streets and the contributing of $685.
600 by the city toward the work.
This report was turned down by the
city council last night.


Following the defeat of the sub
committees report, Councilman Pratt
introduced a motion calling on the
president of the council to appoint a
safety committee with power to plan
legislation for the regulation of the
speed of trains through the city, to
provide gates and watchmen on all
crossings and to consider the propo-
sition of the Sprague avenue and
Division street subway. This reso-
luton was adopted. President koontz
then appointed Councilmen Pratt,
